code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects about an artist into a single fluent sentence.
    Handles a variety of predicates with simple natural‑language templates.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by subject to build richer sentences
    from collections import defaultdict

    subj_map: dict[str, list[Triple]] = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subj_map[t.subject].append(t)

    def clause(subject: str, p: str, o: str) -> str:
        """Return a natural‑language clause for a predicate‑object pair, using the subject when needed."""
        # Predicates that naturally describe the subject
        simple_map = {
            "birthDate": f"was born on {o}",
            "birthYear": f"was born in {o}",
            "deathDate": f"died on {o}",
            "deathPlace": f"died in {o}",
            "origin": f"is from {o}",
            "nationality": f"is {o}",
            "genre": f"performs {o}",
            "instrument": f"plays the {o}",
            "occupation": f"works as {o}",
            "recordLabel": f"is signed to {o}",
            "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": f"is associated with {o}",
            "background": f"has a background as {o}",
            "activeYearsStartYear": f"started their career in {o}",
            "activeYearsEndYear": f"ended their active years in {o}",
        }
        if p in simple_map:
            return simple_map[p]

        # Predicates that relate the subject to another entity
        complex_map = {
            "capital": f"{subject}'s capital is {o}",
            "country": f"{subject} is in {o}",
            "isPartOf": f"{subject} is part of {o}",
            "location": f"{subject} is located in {o}",
            "governingBody": f"{subject} is governed by {o}",
            "leader": f"{subject} is led by {o}",
            "leaderTitle": f"{subject}'s leader holds the title of {o}",
            "language": f"{subject} speaks {o}",
            "officialLanguage": f"{subject} officially uses {o}",
            "currency": f"{subject} uses the {o}",
            "demonym": f"The people of {subject} are called {o}",
            "anthem": f"The anthem of {subject} is {o}",
            "populationDensity": f"{subject} has a population density of {o}",
            "meaning": f"The meaning of {subject} is {o}",
            "longName": f"The long name of {subject} is {o}",
            "areaTotal": f"{subject} covers an area of {o}",
            "foundingDate": f"{subject} was founded on {o}",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": f"{subject} is at an elevation of {o} meters",
            "postalCode": f"The postal code of {subject} is {o}",
        }
        if p in complex_map:
            return complex_map[p]

        # Fallback generic phrasing
        return f"{p} {o}"

    sentences: list[str] = []
    for subject, tlist in subj_map.items():
        clauses = [clause(subject, t.predicate, t.object) for t in tlist]
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            sentences.append(f"{subject} {clauses[0]}")
        else:
            # Join multiple clauses with commas and an 'and' before the last one
            body = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + f", and {clauses[-1]}"
            sentences.append(f"{subject} {body}")

    # Combine all subject‑level sentences into one fluent paragraph‑style sentence
    final_sentence = "; ".join(sentences) + "."
    # Capitalise the first character of the whole output
    return final_sentence[0].upper() + final_sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
